  • Walsh

Walsh¶

The ANF of an n variables Boolean function is a polynomial with n variables and coefficients in GF(2) The ANF of a vectorial Boolean (n,m)-function is a sequence of m ANFs of n variables Boolean functions The univariate representation of an n variables Boolean function is an univariate polynomial with coefficients in GF(2^n) such that its image is in GF(2) The univariate representation of a vectorial Boolean (n,n)-function is an univariate polynomial with coefficients in GF(2^n)

IntToSequence, IntToElt, getConvertionFunction

Maps a to b such that acdot x=Tr(bx)

  1function getDualBasis(n)
  2    K:=GF(2^n);
  3    a:=K.1;
  4    R<x>:=PolynomialRing(K);
  5    f:=R!DefiningPolynomial(K);
  6    fa:=(Evaluate(Derivative(f),a) )^(-1);
  7    gc:=Coefficients(f div (x+a));
  8    B:=[];
  9    for i:=1 to n do
 10        if not IsDefined(gc,i) then
 11            B[i]:=Zero(K);
 12        else
 13            B[i]:=gc[i]*fa;
 14        end if;
 15    end for;
 16    return B;
 17end function;
 18
 19.. code-block:: text
 20:linenos:
 21
 22function getTraceConvertion(D,n)
 23    B:=getDualBasis(n);
 24    trD:=AssociativeArray();
 25    trD[0]:=D[0];
 26    for i:=1 to (2^n-1) do
 27        A:=Eltseq(D[i]);
 28        trD[i]:=&+[B[l]*A[l]: l in [1..n]];
 29    end for;
 30    return trD;
 31end function;
 32
 33.. code-block:: text
 34:linenos:
 35
 36FourierHadamard:=procedure(~H,n : initialIndex:=0)
 37    for i:=1 to n do
 38        for r:=0 to (2^n -1) by 2^i do
 39            t1:=r;
 40            t2:=r+2^(i-1);
 41            for j:=0 to (2^(i-1) -1) do
 42                a:=H[t1+initialIndex];
 43                b:=H[t2+initialIndex];
 44                H[t1+initialIndex]:=a+b;
 45                H[t2+initialIndex]:=a-b;
 46                t1 +:=1;
 47                t2 +:=1;
 48            end for;
 49        end for;
 50    end for;
 51end procedure;
 52
 53.. code-block:: text
 54:linenos:
 55
 56TruthTableToWalshMatrix:=function(TT,n)
 57    m:=#TT;
 58    WF:=ZeroMatrix(Integers(), 2^n, 2^m);
 59    r:=2^n;
 60    c:=2^m;
 61    for i:=1 to r do
 62        WF[i][1]:=1;
 63    end for;
 64    for j:=2 to c do
 65        Sj:=Support(Vector(Intseq(j-1,2,m)));
 66        for i:=1 to r do
 67            u:=Zero(GF(2));
 68            for k in Sj do
 69                u +:=TT[k][i];
 70            end for;
 71            if IsZero(u) then
 72                WF[i][j]:=1;
 73            else
 74                WF[i][j]:=-1;
 75            end if;
 76        end for;
 77    end for;
 78    FourierHadamard(~WF,n : initialIndex:=1);
 79    return WF;
 80end function;
 81
 82.. code-block:: text
 83:linenos:
 84
 85//Walsh transform for Boolean functions
 86bWalsh:=function(f: anf:=false)
 87    Wf:=AssociativeArray();
 88    if anf then
 89        n:=Rank(Parent(f));
 90    else
 91        n:=Degree(BaseRing(Parent(f)));
 92    end if;
 93    q:=2^n;
 94    D:=getConvertionFunction(n: vector:=anf);
 95    for i:=0 to (q-1) do
 96        if IsZero(Evaluate(f,D[i])) then
 97            Wf[i]:=1;
 98        else
 99            Wf[i]:=-1;
100        end if;
101    end for;
102    FourierHadamard(~Wf,n);
103    Wf2:=AssociativeArray();
104    if anf then
105        for i:=0 to (q-1) do
106            Wf2[D[i]]:=Wf[i];
107        end for;
108    else
109        trD:=getTraceConvertion(D,n);
110        for i:=0 to (q-1) do
111            Wf2[trD[i]]:=Wf[i];
112        end for;
113    end if;
114    return Wf2;
115end function;
116
117.. code-block:: text
118:linenos:
119
120//Walsh transform for vectorial Boolean functions
121vWalsh:=function(F:  anf:=false)
122    //vWalsh starts here
123    if anf then
124        m:=#F;
125        n:=Rank(Parent(F[1]));
126        D:=getConvertionFunction(n: vector:=true);
127        if m eq n then
128            cD:=D;
129        else
130            cD:=getConvertionFunction(m : vector:=true);
131        end if;
132        TT:=[([Evaluate(F[j],D[i]): i in [0..(2^n-1)]]): j in [1..m]];
133    else
134        K:=BaseRing(Parent(F));
135        a:=K.1;
136        m:=Degree(K);
137        n:=m;
138        D:=getConvertionFunction(n);
139        TT:=[([Trace(a^j *Evaluate(F,D[i])): i in [0..(2^n-1)]]): j in [0..(n-1)]];
140    end if;
141    WFm:=TruthTableToWalshMatrix(TT,n);
142    WF:=AssociativeArray();
143    if anf then
144        for i:=0 to (2^n-1) do
145            Di:=D[i];
146            WF[Di]:=AssociativeArray();
147            for j:=0 to (2^m-1) do
148                WF[Di][cD[j]]:=WFm[i+1][j+1];
149            end for;
150        end for;
151    else
152        trD:=getTraceConvertion(D,n);
153        for i:=0 to (2^n-1) do
154            trDi:=trD[i];
155            WF[trDi]:=AssociativeArray();
156            for j:=0 to (2^m-1) do
157                WF[trDi][D[j]]:=WFm[i+1][j+1];
158            end for;
159        end for;
160    end if;
161    return WF;
162end function;
163
164.. code-block:: text
165:linenos:
166
167TruthTableToBoolean:=function(TT)
168    n:=Integers()!Log(2,#TT);
169    R<[x]>:=PolynomialRing(GF(2),n);
170    f:=Zero(R);
171    f+:= TT[1];
172    for i:=1 to (2^n-1) do
173        ai:=Zero(GF(2));
174        Si:=Support(Vector(IntToSequence(i,n)));
175        for I in Subsets(Si) do
176            if IsEmpty(I) then
177                ai +:=TT[1];
178            else
179                k:=&+[2^(j-1): j in I]+1;
180                ai +:=TT[k];
181            end if;
182        end for;
183        if IsOne(ai) then
184            f+:=(&*[x[j]: j in Si]);
185        end if;
186    end for;
187    return f;
188end function;
189
190.. code-block:: text
191:linenos:
192
193InverseVectorialWalsh:=function(WF : anf:=false)
194    n:=Integers()!Log(2,#Keys(WF));
195    D:=getConvertionFunction(n :vector:=anf);
196    WFc:=AssociativeArray();
197    if anf then
198        m:=Integers()!Log(2,#Keys(WF[Rep(Keys(WF))]));
199        for j:=1 to m do
200            vj:=[Zero(GF(2)): k in [1..m]];
201            vj[j]:=One(GF(2));
202            WFc[j]:=AssociativeArray();
203            for i:=0 to (2^n-1) do
204                WFc[j][i]:=WF[D[i]][vj];
205            end for;
206            FourierHadamard(~WFc[j],n);
207        end for;
208        return [TruthTableToBoolean([GF(2)!((1- (WFc[j][i] div 2^n) ) div 2): i in [0..(2^n-1)]]): j in [1..m]];
209    else
210        m:=n;
211        K:=GF(2^n);
212        a:=K.1;
213        B:=getDualBasis(n);
214        WFc:=AssociativeArray();
215        trD:=getTraceConvertion(D,n);
216        for j:=1 to n do
217            vj:=a^(j-1);
218            WFc[j]:=AssociativeArray();
219            for i:=0 to (2^n-1) do
220                WFc[j][i]:=WF[trD[i]][vj];
221            end for;
222            FourierHadamard(~WFc[j],n);
223        end for;
224        return Interpolation([D[i]: i in [0..(2^n-1)]],[ &+[((1-(WFc[j][i] div 2^n)) div 2)*B[j] : j in [1..n]]: i in [0..(2^n-1)]]);
225    end if;
226end function;
